description_long: This is a modern civilian helicopter with a streamlined fuselage painted primarily in white and blue, featuring a sleek aerodynamic design. The helicopter has a large, rounded cockpit with expansive, dark-tinted windows that wrap around the front and sides, providing excellent visibility for pilots and passengers. The main rotor consists of four long, black blades, while the tail section includes a fenestron-style enclosed tail rotor for enhanced safety and noise reduction. The landing gear comprises two sturdy, metallic skids with crossbars for stability during ground contact. Distinctive markings, including the registration number 'NS016', are visible on the tail boom. The overall appearance suggests a lightweight yet robust construction suitable for urban air mobility, emergency response, or executive transport.
description: A sleek, white and blue civilian helicopter with large tinted cockpit windows, four-blade main rotor, enclosed tail rotor, and metallic landing skids.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the helicopter, main and tail rotors, cockpit windows, and registration number on the tail.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the rounded cockpit, expansive windows, and the alignment of the main rotor blades.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the tail boom, skids, and the fenestron tail rotor, with the registration number visible.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the tail section, enclosed tail rotor, and the symmetry of the landing skids.
attribute_rotor_blades: Four long, straight, black main rotor blades made of composite material, each with a slight taper and no visible markings.
attribute_tail_rotor: Fenestron-style enclosed tail rotor with multiple short blades, oriented vertically within the tail fin, colored blue and white with no visible markings.
attribute_landing_skids: Two parallel metallic landing skids, painted gray with black footpads, connected by crossbars for stability.
attribute_cockpit_windows: Five large, dark-tinted cockpit windows: one front, two side, and two smaller lower panels, all with black framing and smooth curves.
